prose:
**Fukui reactivity analysis of nitrobenzene** — DFT, tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**), computed with PySCF (dft backend), **gas phase**, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Density fitting was OFF (exact four-center integrals; chemkit default). The N−1 (cation) and N+1 (anion) states were each computed as doublets (charge +1/−1, multiplicity 2), derived automatically from the singlet parent. Fukui functions are based on Mulliken (PySCF) partial charges via the finite-difference (ΔN = ±1) scheme. Molecule: 14 atoms (nitrobenzene, C6H5NO2). Geometry: taken as-is from the user-supplied file `nitrobenzene.xyz` — this was a set of single-point SCF calculations on that fixed input geometry; no geometry optimization was performed at this level.

**Headline result — most electrophilic site (largest f⁺):** atom index 4 (1-based), a **carbon**, with **f⁺ = 0.49642**. The most nucleophilic site (largest f⁻) is atom index 5 (1-based), a carbon, with f⁻ = 0.107288.

Full per-atom descriptors (order: O, O, N, C, C, C, C, C, C, H, H, H, H, H):
- f⁺ (electrophilic attack): [0.47317, 0.47344, −0.45670, 0.49642, 0.11563, 0.11588, 0.17412, 0.17423, 0.28914, −0.23169, −0.23182, −0.13372, −0.13379, −0.12431]
- f⁻ (nucleophilic attack): [0.097016, 0.096774, 0.017243, −0.014292, 0.107288, 0.107237, 0.094922, 0.094892, 0.008312, 0.075212, 0.075203, 0.083883, 0.083882, 0.072428]
- f⁰ (radical): [0.28509, 0.28511, −0.21973, 0.24106, 0.11146, 0.11156, 0.13452, 0.13456, 0.14872, −0.078239, −0.078307, −0.024921, −0.024956, −0.025939]
- dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻): [0.37616, 0.37667, −0.47394, 0.51071, 0.008338, 0.008645, 0.079202, 0.079337, 0.28082, −0.30690, −0.30702, −0.21761, −0.21768, −0.19673]

**Integrity / convergence:** the engine reports `integrity.status = "ok"` and **`trustworthy = true`**. Integrity checks all passed: charge conservation for f⁺ (sum = 1.0000) and f⁻ (sum = 1.0000), and schema header present. **No warnings were reported** (the result `warnings` array is empty; the only messages are the passing integrity-check details, which note that a large drift in the sum-to-1 would indicate a non-converged N±1 state — here both sums are exactly 1.0000).

**Method note:** This is a semi-local finite-difference Fukui analysis built on Mulliken charges, which are basis-set-dependent; the descriptors indicate relative site reactivity trends rather than absolute quantities. No experimental or literature comparison is offered.

**Reproducibility:** command `cli.py fukui --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard nitrobenzene.xyz`. A bar-chart PNG of f⁺/f⁻/dual per atom was written to `.../20260706-183139_nitrobenzene_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`.
